author: Jelena Martinovic
teaser: TICAL Official, a cannabis brands led by Method Man, a rapper, actor and entrepreneur, announced on Friday its adult-use availability in Illinois. That's in partnership with Flora Arbor LLC, launching with the Judgement Day, Love Jones, and The Method lyrical edition strains."Illinois is the third largest cannabis market in the US and we're honored to be the exclusive manufacturer and distributor of TICAL Official products in Illinois," Anthony Carpino, CEO of Flora Arbor, said. "We plan on launching TICAL with Black-owned, minority-owned, and female-owned dispensaries."
teaserText: TICAL Official, a cannabis brands led by Method Man, a rapper, actor and entrepreneur, announced on Friday its adult-use availability in Illinois. That's in partnership with Flora Arbor LLC, launching with the Judgement Day, Love Jones, and The Method lyrical edition strains."Illinois is the third largest cannabis market in the US and we're honored to be the exclusive manufacturer and distributor of TICAL Official products in Illinois," Anthony Carpino, CEO of Flora Arbor, said. "We plan on launching TICAL with Black-owned, minority-owned, and female-owned dispensaries."
title: Method Man's Weed Products Arrive In Illinois, Here's Where To Find Them

assets.alt: The Return Of The Everlasting Denim Craze Was Not Enough To Boost Levi's Sales

author: Upwallstreet
teaser: On Wednesday, Levi Strauss & Co. (NYSE: LEVI) reported its fiscal second quarter results. While Levi managed to top Wall Street estimates on the earnings front due to its direct sales channel and cost cutting initiatives, revenue fell short. Upon the report, shares fell about 15% during extended trading.
teaserText: On Wednesday, Levi Strauss & Co. (NYSE: LEVI) reported its fiscal second quarter results. While Levi managed to top Wall Street estimates on the earnings front due to its direct sales channel and cost cutting initiatives, revenue fell short. Upon the report, shares fell about 15% during extended trading.
title: The Return Of The Everlasting Denim Craze Was Not Enough To Boost Levi's Sales
